description: Local development overrides or test data unintentionally written to a shared or production-like backend instead of a local sandbox.
body: Failure mode: a dev run points at a shared database, queue, or service because the default config or an env var was not overridden locally, so local experiments mutate data other people or production depend on. Detection signals: local actions appear in shared dashboards; connection strings point at non-local hosts; test data shows up in a shared store. Do not run local dev against shared/production backends by default. Safer fallback: default local config to local services, require explicit opt-in to point at any shared environment, and verify the target host before write operations. Risk boundary: escalate and confirm with a human before any local run can write to a shared or production datastore.
